title: Velvety Argentinian Cream Soup
description: A buttery, creamy, light Argentinian soup featuring garlic and a smooth finish, kosher‑friendly and perfect for an elegant lunch.
instructions: Heat a large pot over medium heat and melt the butter.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic, sautéing until fragrant and translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the diced potatoes and cook for an additional 2 minutes, coating them with butter.
Pour in the chicken stock, bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to a simmer.
Simmer uncovered for 15 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender when pierced with a fork.
Add the crushed thyme, salt, pepper, and a pinch of nutmeg; stir to combine.
Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until silky smooth, or transfer to a blender in batches.
Return the soup to the pot, stir in the heavy cream, and heat through for 3 minutes, keeping it below boiling.
Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary; if the soup is too thick, add a splash of stock or water.
Serve hot, garnished with a swirl of cream and a sprinkle of fresh thyme or chopped parsley.
